answer:
**The Falsafa corpus does not contain a direct explanation or discussion of *parināma* (transformation or evolution) within any "tattva texts" (works focused on *tattva*, or metaphysical principles of reality).**

Searches for "parinama", "pariṇāma", and related terms across English translations yield only two matches, both in the Buddhist-influenced *San Hyan Kamahayanikan* (ch. 1), where *pariṇāmaduḥkatā* refers to "suffering-of-change"—one of three kinds of suffering (*duḥkhatā*): suffering-of-suffering (*duḥkhaduḥkatā*), suffering-of-fabrication (*saṃskāraduḥkatā*), and suffering-of-change (*pariṇāmaduḥkatā*).[^1] This usage aligns with standard Mahāyāna Buddhist doctrine (e.g., impermanence leading to suffering) but is unrelated to *tattva* frameworks.

The corpus does include several works touching on *tattva* (principles of reality), primarily Old Javanese Śaiva texts:
- *San Hyan Tattvajnana* (Divine Knowledge of Reality), which defines *tattvajñāna* as the root of all *tattvas*, distinguishing *cetana* (conscious, Śivatattva) from *acetana* (unconscious, Māyātattva).[^2]
- *San Hyan Mahājñāna* (Sacred Supreme Wisdom), which invokes *tattva* repeatedly as ultimate reality, *oṃkāra*, or Śiva's essence.[^3]
- *Gaṇapatitattva*, a brief text on Gaṇeśa-related purity and *tattva*.[^4]

No Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta, Kashmir Śaivite, or Sāṃkhya texts (e.g., on 36 *tattvas* or *parināma*-vāda vs. *vivarta*) appear to discuss *parināma* in a *tattva* context. The corpus's 37 works emphasize smṛti (e.g., *Manusmṛti*), Javanese tantric, and other Indic traditions but lack comprehensive coverage of this specific concept.

[^1]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, ch. 1 ([p-39e8bd](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-39e8bd)).

[^2]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch. 1 ([p-d53c24](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-d53c24); [p-daaeef](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef)).

[^3]: *San Hyan Mahājñāna*, ch. 1 (multiple refs., e.g., [p-0f17df](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-0f17df)).

[^4]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, ch. 1 ([p-c23162](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-c23162)).
